2605.05833.title: On the Role of Language Representations in Auto-Bidding: Findings and Implications
2605.05833.paper_summary: Auto-bidding is a crucial task in real-time advertising markets, where policies must optimize long-horizon value under delivery constraints (e.g., budget and CPA). Existing methods for auto-bidding rely on compact numerical state representations: while they can implicitly capture delivery dynamics, they offer limited support for explicitly representing and controlling high-level intent, evolving feedback, and operator-style strategic guidance in real campaigns. Meanwhile, Large Language Models (LLMs) offer a powerful method for encoding semantic information, it remains unclear when LLMs help and how to integrate them without sacrificing numerical precision. Through systematic preliminary studies, we find that (1) LLM embeddings contain bidding-relevant cues yet cannot replace numerical features, and (2) gains emerge only with careful semantic--numeric integration rather than naive concatenation. Motivated by these findings, we propose \textit{SemBid}, a novel auto-bidding framework that injects LLM-encoded semantics into offline bidding trajectories at the token level. SemBid introduces three semantic inputs: \textit{Task}, \textit{History}, and \textit{Strategy}. It injects these semantics as tokens alongside numerical trajectory tokens and uses self-attention to integrate them, improving controllability and generalization across objectives. Across diverse scenarios and budget regimes, SemBid outperforms competitive baselines from offline RL and generative sequence modeling, with more consistent gains in overall performance, constraint satisfaction, and robustness. Our code is available at: \href{https://github.com/AlanYu04/SemBid-KDD2026}{\textcolor{blue}{here}}.
2605.05833.paper_summary_zh: 自動出價是在即時廣告市場中一項關鍵任務，政策必須在交付限制（例如預算和CPA）下優化長期價值。現有的自動出價方法依賴於緊湊的數值狀態表示：雖然它們可以隱式捕捉交付動態，但對於明確表示和控制高層意圖、演變反饋以及在真實活動中的操作風格戰略指導的支持有限。與此同時，大型語言模型（LLMs）提供了一種強大的編碼語義信息的方法，但尚不清楚LLMs何時有助於自動出價，以及如何整合它們而不犧牲數值精度。通過系統的初步研究，我們發現（1）LLM嵌入包含與出價相關的線索，但無法替代數值特徵，以及（2）增益僅在小心的語義-數值整合中出現，而不是簡單的串接。受到這些發現的啟發，我們提出了\textit{SemBid}，一種新穎的自動出價框架，將LLM編碼的語義注入到離線出價軌跡中，並在標記層級進行處理。SemBid引入了三個語義輸入：\textit{Task}、\textit{History}和\textit{Strategy}。它將這些語義作為標記與數值軌跡標記一起注入，並使用自我注意力進行整合，提高了可控性和跨目標的泛化能力。在各種場景和預算範疇中，SemBid超越了來自離線強化學習和生成序列建模的競爭基準，並在整體性能、約束滿足和穩健性方面獲得了更一致的增益。我們的代碼可在：\href{https://github.com/AlanYu04/SemBid-KDD2026}{\textcolor{blue}{這裡}}獲得。
2605.05833.author: Guanyu Zhu et.al.
2605.05833.authors: Guanyu Zhu, Jining Luan, Hanwen Du, Xinyu Fang, Sibo Xu, Ersheng Ni, Hongji Li, Jincheng Fang, Ronghao Chen, Huacan Wang, Xuanqi Lan, Yongxin Ni, Yiqi Sun, Youhua Li
